refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness, and environmental factors. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time achievement does not compromise safety constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥30 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, ambient conditions). Validate against CT_target via telemetry data logged by the NGHE solution. Confirm that safety-critical functions (e.g., load stability, emergency stop) do not degrade cycle time performance.
sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations with repeatable cycle time variance (p95 - p50) ≤ ΔCT_acceptable.
FR-CT-002: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness (command latency ≤ L_max) sufficient to achieve CT_target without safety compromise.
FR-CT-003: The NGHE solution shall log and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, phase timestamps) with ≥99% data integrity.
FR-CT-004: Safety services shall not introduce cycle time penalties that exceed ΔCT_safety_budget.

candidate_dps: DP-CT-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic system response time and operator control loop latency via hardware tuning and real-time control firmware.
DP-CT-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and adaptive duty cycle scheduling to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target under variable site conditions.
DP-CT-C: Establish a closed-loop cycle time monitoring and feedback system that continuously measures phase durations and alerts operators to deviations.
DP-CT-D: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, and dump sub-phases; allocate performance budgets to each phase and assign ownership to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-CT-D: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, and dump sub-phases; allocate performance budgets to each phase and assign ownership to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. This DP is selected because it enables hierarchical functional decomposition, explicit interface contracts between modules, and risk-driven allocation of cycle time budgets to safety-critical and performance-critical subsystems.

potential_sources: Vehicle hydraulic response time couples with teleoperation command latency; both must be co-optimized to achieve CT_target.
Safety services (load stability monitoring, emergency stop) may introduce latency that couples with vehicle control loop; safety budget allocation is critical.
Telemetry logging and reporting may introduce minor latency in real-time control; buffering and asynchronous logging required.
coupling_check.notes: DP-CT-D is a decomposition strategy that does not itself introduce coupling; rather, it exposes coupling between sub-phases and enables explicit management via budget allocation and interface contracts. Coupling is acceptable if budgets are respected and interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are well-defined and stable; risk: if customer changes target or duty cycle mid-project, budget allocation must be revisited.
Assumption: Vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services can be independently optimized within allocated budgets; risk: if one subsystem cannot meet its budget, cascading re-allocation required.

Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure has sufficient bandwidth and latency to capture cycle time data without introducing control loop delays; risk: if telemetry is synchronous with control, latency penalty may be unacceptable.
Risk: Safety criticality may force conservative design choices (e.g., slower load acceleration) that conflict with cycle time targets; mitigation required via explicit safety budget.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-CT-D to explicitly define the cycle time budget allocation mechanism: (1) specify how CT_target is decomposed into load, travel, dump budgets; (2) define the interface contract between vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services (e.g., maximum latency, minimum throughput); (3) establish a change control process for budget re-allocation if subsystem performance changes.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. At this level, the IM is responsible for: (1) defining and enforcing cycle time budgets across sub-modules; (2) coordinating telemetry collection and reporting; (3) ensuring that safety constraints do not violate cycle time targets; (4) providing a unified interface to the Site Manager and Equipment Operator for cycle time monitoring and performance feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exports cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, p95 statistics, performance alerts) to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality for KPI tracking and diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, ambient conditions) and CT_target from Site Manager to establish performance budgets.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exports safety-related cycle time alerts (e.g., load stability warnings, emergency stop triggers) to Safety Manager and Operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to coordinate teleoperation commands, telemetry synchronization, and safety alerts between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for cycle time KPI management. It owns the budget allocation logic, telemetry aggregation, and cross-module coordination. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry export and tasking input cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns the load and dump sub-phases (hydraulic response, mechanical actuation). However, cycle time KPI management (budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, safety coordination) must reside at NGHE Solution level. If cycle time logic is embedded in Vehicle, it will diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, violating separation of concerns.

Teleoperation owns the travel sub-phase and operator control latency. However, cycle time KPI management is a system-level concern that must be coordinated across Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services. Embedding cycle time logic in Teleoperation would create tight coupling and diffusion.

Safety Services owns safety-critical constraints (load stability, emergency stop) that impact cycle time budgets. However, Safety Services should not own the cycle time KPI itself; rather, it should provide safety constraints that inform budget allocation at the NGHE Solution level. Embedding cycle time logic in Safety Services would violate the separation between safety constraints and performance optimization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of cycle time KPI management because: (1) it is the top-level system boundary that integrates all sub-modules; (2) it has visibility into the full load→travel→dump cycle; (3) it can coordinate budget allocation and telemetry aggregation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services without creating diffusion; (4) it can enforce safety constraints without compromising performance optimization.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god object' that owns too many concerns (cycle time, safety, telemetry, tasking), it may become difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Define clear responsibility boundaries between NGHE Solution and sub-modules via explicit interface contracts.

Risk: If cycle time budgets are not enforced at the NGHE Solution level, sub-modules may independently optimize for their own performance, leading to sub-optimal system-level cycle time. Mitigation: Establish a budget allocation mechanism and change control process at the NGHE Solution level.

Risk: If telemetry collection is synchronous with control loops, it may introduce latency that violates cycle time targets. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous telemetry logging and buffering at the NGHE Solution level.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define: (1) the cycle time budget allocation mechanism (how CT_target is decomposed into load, travel, dump budgets); (2) the interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services (e.g., maximum latency, minimum throughput, telemetry format); (3) the telemetry aggregation and reporting logic (how phase timestamps are collected, p95 statistics computed, and alerts generated); (4) the change control process for budget re-allocation if subsystem performance changes.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target with telemetry monitoring) is directly addressed by the selected DP (decompose cycle time into sub-phases and allocate budgets). The DP is a logical solution strategy that enables hierarchical decomposition and explicit interface contracts. The FR is verifiable via telemetry data and cycle time measurements. No FR-DP mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If cycle time budget allocation logic is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, it will create diffusion and make budget enforcement difficult. Mitigation: Centralize budget allocation at NGHE Solution level.

Risk: If telemetry collection is implemented independently in each sub-module, it will create redundancy and synchronization issues. Mitigation: Implement telemetry aggregation at NGHE Solution level with asynchronous logging.

Risk: If safety constraints are not explicitly communicated to NGHE Solution, cycle time budgets may be violated by safety-critical operations. Mitigation: Define explicit safety budget allocation at NGHE Solution level.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution IM boundary is appropriate for containing the cycle time KPI management logic. The boundary is clear: NGHE Solution owns budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and cross-module coordination; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective sub-phases and constraints. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not create diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR is verifiable via telemetry data exported by NGHE Solution. Cycle time measurements (p95 ≤ CT_target) can be computed from phase timestamps logged during operation. Verification is feasible if telemetry infrastructure is reliable and has sufficient bandwidth.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of cycle time KPI to NGHE Solution is clear. NGHE Solution is responsible for: (1) defining and enforcing cycle time budgets; (2) collecting and aggregating telemetry; (3) reporting p95 statistics to Site Manager; (4) coordinating with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to ensure budgets are met. Sub-modules are responsible for meeting their allocated budgets within their respective sub-phases.

recommended_revisions: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define the cycle time budget allocation mechanism and interface contracts with sub-modules.
Establish a telemetry infrastructure design that ensures asynchronous logging and minimal latency impact on control loops.
Define a safety budget allocation process that ensures safety constraints do not violate cycle time targets.
Establish a change control process for budget re-allocation if subsystem performance changes or customer requirements change.

decision.justification: The refined FR-CT (cycle time KPI) is not atomic and requires further decomposition into sub-FRs (load, travel, dump sub-phases; teleoperation latency; telemetry logging; safety budget). The selected DP (decompose cycle time into sub-phases and allocate budgets) is a decomposition strategy that naturally leads to hierarchical functional decomposition. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate for the current level but requires refinement of internal structure and interface contracts. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is justified to allocate cycle time budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services and to define explicit interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations with repeatable cycle time variance (p95 - p50) ≤ ΔCT_acceptable.
FR-CT-002: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness (command latency ≤ L_max) sufficient to achieve CT_target without safety compromise.
FR-CT-003: The NGHE solution shall log and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, phase timestamps) with ≥99% data integrity.
FR-CT-004: Safety services shall not introduce cycle time penalties that exceed ΔCT_safety_budget.

assumptions: CT_target and duty cycle are well-defined and stable.
Vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services can be independently optimized within allocated budgets.
Telemetry infrastructure has sufficient bandwidth and latency to capture cycle time data without introducing control loop delays.
Safety criticality can be managed via explicit safety budget allocation without compromising cycle time targets.
p95 cycle time is an appropriate KPI for customer acceptance; alternative metrics (e.g., mean, p90) are not required.
open_risks: Risk: If customer changes CT_target or duty cycle mid-project, budget allocation must be revisited; impact on schedule and cost unknown.
Risk: If one subsystem cannot meet its allocated budget, cascading re-allocation required; feasibility of re-allocation unknown.
Risk: Safety criticality may force conservative design choices that conflict with cycle time targets; mitigation strategy not yet defined.
Risk: Telemetry infrastructure may introduce latency that violates cycle time targets; design of asynchronous logging not yet detailed.
evidence_needed: Detailed definition of CT_target and duty cycle (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, ambient conditions).
Baseline cycle time measurements from existing equipment or prototypes to inform budget allocation.
Safety criticality assessment and safety budget allocation strategy.
Telemetry infrastructure design and latency analysis to confirm minimal impact on control loops.
Interface contract definitions between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services (latency, throughput, data format).
